ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Does subject ever get confused whether something they have experienced is real or imaginary?
Do familiar people or surroundings ever feel strange, confusing or unreal to subject?
Has subject ever felt that they're not in control of their own ideas or thoughts?
Has subject ever felt that their ability to think has changed in any way?
Does subject ever feel that thoughts are put into their head or taken away? That some person or force is interfering with their thinking?
Does subject ever feel as if their thoughts are being said out loud so that other people can hear them?
Does subject ever think they can read other people's minds or that others can read their mind?
Does subject ever feel that the radio, TV, computer, cell phone or other device is communicating directly to them?
Does subject ever think they can predict the future?
Does subject ever feel that people around them are thinking about them in a negative way?
Has subject ever found themself feeling mistrustful or suspicious of other people?
Does subject ever feel like they are being singled out or watched?
Does subject ever feel that people might be intending to harm you?
Does subject feel that they have special gifts, talents or powers?
Do people tell subject that their plans or goals are unrealistic? If so, what are those plans or goals?
Does subject ever think of themself as a famous or person or believe that they have a relationship with a famous person or with God?
Does subjects' ears ever play tricks on them or do they ever hear sounds like banging, clicking, hissing, ringing in their ears, footsteps or their name being called?
Does subject ever hear a voice that others don't seem to hear?
Does subject ever see things like flashes, flames, vague figures, shadows or movement out of the corner of their eye?
Does subject ever see things that others can't or don't seem to see?
Has subject ever noticed any bodily sensations or do they smell or taste things that others don't notice?
Do people ever tell subject that they can't understand when subject speaks?
Do you ever have trouble getting your point across like rambling or going off track?
Do you ever completely lose your train of thought?
Description of Worst Delusion-Like Symptoms (DEL)
Delusion-Like Symptoms (DEL) Range
Notes: 0= Normal Range; 1= APS Range; 2= Psychotic Range

ElementDescription: Description of Worst Hallucination-Like Symptoms (HAL)
Hallucination-Like Symptoms (HAL) Range

ElementDescription: Description of Worst Disorganized Communication (DIS)
Disorganized Communication (DIS) Range
Notes: 0= Normal Range; 1= APS Range; 2= Psychotic Range

ElementDescription: Have any of the 3 symptoms EVER been in the psychotic range?
Are psychotic symptom(s) seriously disorganizing or dangerous, or have they EVER been?
Did the symptom(s) EVER occur in the psychotic range for at least one hour per day at an average frequency of four days per week over one month?
APS Rating-DEL: Are Delusion-Like Symptoms (DEL) CURRENTLY in the Attenuated Psychosis Syndrome (APS) Range?
APS Rating-DEL: Do Delusion-Like Symptoms (DEL) CURRENTLY occur more than or equal to 1x/week?
APS Rating-DEL: Do Delusion-Like Symptoms (DEL) bother the patient or interfere with their daily activities?
APS Rating-DEL: Are all 3 boxes immediately above checked YES?
APS Rating-HAL: Are Hallucination-Like Symptoms (HAL) CURRENTLY in the Attenuated Psychosis Syndrome (APS) Range?
APS Rating-HAL: Do Hallucination-Like Symptoms (HAL) CURRENTLY occur more than or equal to 1x/week?
APS Rating-HAL: Do Hallucination-Like Symptoms (HAL) bother the patient or interfere with their daily activities?
APS Rating-HAL: Are all 3 boxes immediately above checked YES?
APS Rating-DIS: Are Disorganized Communication (DIS) CURRENTLY in the Attenuated Psychosis Syndrome (APS) Range?
APS Rating-DIS: Do Disorganized Communication (DIS) CURRENTLY occur more than or equal to 1x/week?
APS Rating-DIS: Do Disorganized Communication (DIS) bother the patient or interfere with their daily activities?
APS Rating-DIS: Are all 3 boxes immediately above checked YES?
APS Diagnosis: Does participant qualify for Attenuated Psychosis Syndrome (APS)?
APS Diagnosis: Why does participant not qualify for Attenuated Psychosis Syndrome (APS) diagnosis?
Notes: 0= Not on psychosis spectrum; 1= Full psychosis

ElementDescription: Comments about assessment
